title: rosa scabrata f. ovifera
intro: Rosa scabrata f. ovifera is a lesser-known variant of the Rosa scabrata species, commonly referred to as a wild rose with unique oviform hips. The plant's hips, rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The hips of Rosa scabrata f. ovifera are said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the hips are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
